NKJV.1: Then all the tax collectors and the sinners drew near to Him to hear Him.
NKJV.2: And the Pharisees and scribes complained, saying, “This Man receives sinners and eats with them.”
NKJV.3: So He spoke this parable to them, saying:
NKJV.4: “What man of you, having a hundred sheep, if he loses one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness, and go after the one which is lost until he finds it?
NKJV.5: And when he has found it, he lays it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
NKJV.6: And when he comes home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep which was lost!’
NKJV.7: I say to you that likewise there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents  than over ninety-nine just persons who  need no repentance.
NKJV.8: “Or what woman, having ten silver coins, if she loses one coin, does not light a lamp, sweep the house, and search carefully until she finds it?
NKJV.9: And when she has found it, she calls her friends and neighbors together, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found the piece which I lost!’
NKJV.10: Likewise, I say to you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner who repents.”
NKJV.11: Then He said: “A certain man had two sons.
NKJV.12: And the younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me the portion of goods that falls to me.’ So he divided to them his livelihood.
NKJV.13: And not many days after, the younger son gathered all together, journeyed to a far country, and there wasted his possessions with prodigal living.
NKJV.14: But when he had spent all, there arose a severe famine in that land, and he began to be in want.
NKJV.15: Then he went and joined himself to a citizen of that country, and he sent him into his fields to feed swine.
NKJV.16: And he would gladly have filled his stomach with the pods that the swine ate, and no one gave him anything.
NKJV.17: “But when he came to himself, he said, ‘How many of my father’s hired servants have bread enough and to spare, and I perish with hunger!
NKJV.18: I will arise and go to my father, and will say to him, “Father,  I have sinned against heaven and before you,
NKJV.19: and I am no longer worthy to be called your son. Make me like one of your hired servants.” ’
NKJV.20: “And he arose and came to his father. But  when he was still a great way off, his father saw him and had compassion, and ran and fell on his neck and kissed him.
NKJV.21: And the son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven  and in your sight, and am no longer worthy to be called your son.’
NKJV.22: “But the father said to his servants, ‘Bring out the best robe and put it on him, and put a ring on his hand and sandals on his feet.
NKJV.23: And bring the fatted calf here and kill it, and let us eat and be merry;
NKJV.24: for this my son was dead and is alive again; he was lost and is found.’ And they began to be merry.
NKJV.25: “Now his older son was in the field. And as he came and drew near to the house, he heard music and dancing.
NKJV.26: So he called one of the servants and asked what these things meant.
NKJV.27: And he said to him, ‘Your brother has come, and because he has received him safe and sound, your father has killed the fatted calf.’
NKJV.28: “But he was angry and would not go in. Therefore his father came out and pleaded with him.
NKJV.29: So he answered and said to his father, ‘Lo, these many years I have been serving you; I never transgressed your commandment at any time; and yet you never gave me a young goat, that I might make merry with my friends.
NKJV.30: But as soon as this son of yours came, who has devoured your livelihood with harlots, you killed the fatted calf for him.’
NKJV.31: “And he said to him, ‘Son, you are always with me, and all that I have is yours.
NKJV.32: It was right that we should make merry and be glad,  for your brother was dead and is alive again, and was lost and is found.’ ”

ESV.1: Now the tax collectors and sinners were all drawing near to hear him.
ESV.2: And the Pharisees and the scribes grumbled, saying, “This man receives sinners and eats with them.”
ESV.3: So he told them this parable:
ESV.4: “What man of you, having a hundred sheep, if he has lost one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the open country, and go after the one that is lost, until he finds it?
ESV.5: And when he has found it, he lays it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
ESV.6: And when he comes home, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep that was lost.’
ESV.7: Just so, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous persons who need no repentance.
ESV.8: “Or what woman, having ten silver coins,  if she loses one coin, does not light a lamp and sweep the house and seek diligently until she finds it?
ESV.9: And when she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found the coin that I had lost.’
ESV.10: Just so, I tell you, there is joy before the angels of God over one sinner who repents.”
ESV.11: And he said, “There was a man who had two sons.
ESV.12: And the younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me the share of property that is coming to me.’ And he divided his property between them.
ESV.13: Not many days later, the younger son gathered all he had and took a journey into a far country, and there he squandered his property in reckless living.
ESV.14: And when he had spent everything, a severe famine arose in that country, and he began to be in need.
ESV.15: So he went and hired himself out to  one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
ESV.16: And he was longing to be fed with the pods that the pigs ate, and no one gave him anything.
ESV.17: “But when he came to himself, he said, ‘How many of my father's hired servants have more than enough bread, but I perish here with hunger!
ESV.18: I will arise and go to my father, and I will say to him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven and before you.
ESV.19: I am no longer worthy to be called your son. Treat me as one of your hired servants.”’
ESV.20: And he arose and came to his father. But while he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt compassion, and ran and embraced him and kissed him.
ESV.21: And the son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and before you. I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’
ESV.22: But the father said to his servants,  ‘Bring quickly the best robe, and put it on him, and put a ring on his hand, and shoes on his feet.
ESV.23: And bring the fattened calf and kill it, and let us eat and celebrate.
ESV.24: For this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found.’ And they began to celebrate.
ESV.25: “Now his older son was in the field, and as he came and drew near to the house, he heard music and dancing.
ESV.26: And he called one of the servants and asked what these things meant.
ESV.27: And he said to him, ‘Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fattened calf, because he has received him back safe and sound.’
ESV.28: But he was angry and refused to go in. His father came out and entreated him,
ESV.29: but he answered his father, ‘Look, these many years I have served you, and I never disobeyed your command, yet you never gave me a young goat, that I might celebrate with my friends.
ESV.30: But when this son of yours came, who has devoured your property with prostitutes, you killed the fattened calf for him!’
ESV.31: And he said to him, ‘Son, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.
ESV.32: It was fitting to celebrate and be glad, for this your brother was dead, and is alive; he was lost, and is found.’”

NASB.1: Now all the tax collectors and the sinners were coming near Him to listen to Him.
NASB.2: Both the Pharisees and the scribes [began] to grumble, saying, 'This man receives sinners and eats with them.'
NASB.3: So He told them this parable, saying,
NASB.4: 'What man among you, if he has a hundred sheep and has lost one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the open pasture and go after the one which is lost until he finds it?
NASB.5: 'When he has found it, he lays it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
NASB.6: 'And when he comes home, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, 'Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep which was lost!'
NASB.7: 'I tell you that in the same way, there will be [more] joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous persons who need no repentance.
NASB.8: 'Or what woman, if she has ten silver coins and loses one coin, does not light a lamp and sweep the house and search carefully until she finds it?
NASB.9: 'When she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, 'Rejoice with me, for I have found the coin which I had lost!'
NASB.10: 'In the same way, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner who repents.'
NASB.11: And He said, 'A man had two sons.
NASB.12: 'The younger of them said to his father, 'Father, give me the share of the estate that falls to me.' So he divided his wealth between them.
NASB.13: 'And not many days later, the younger son gathered everything together and went on a journey into a distant country, and there he squandered his estate with loose living.
NASB.14: 'Now when he had spent everything, a severe famine occurred in that country, and he began to be impoverished.
NASB.15: 'So he went and hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, and he sent him into his fields to feed swine.
NASB.16: 'And he would have gladly filled his stomach with the pods that the swine were eating, and no one was giving [anything] to him.
NASB.17: 'But when he came to his senses, he said, 'How many of my father's hired men have more than enough bread, but I am dying here with hunger!
NASB.18: 'I will get up and go to my father, and will say to him, 'Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in your sight;
NASB.19: I am no longer worthy to be called your son; make me as one of your hired men.''
NASB.20: 'So he got up and came to his father. But while he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt compassion [for him], and ran and embraced him and kissed him.
NASB.21: 'And the son said to him, 'Father, I have sinned against heaven and in your sight; I am no longer worthy to be called your son.'
NASB.22: 'But the father said to his slaves, 'Quickly bring out the best robe and put it on him, and put a ring on his hand and sandals on his feet;
NASB.23: and bring the fattened calf, kill it, and let us eat and celebrate;
NASB.24: for this son of mine was dead and has come to life again; he was lost and has been found.' And they began to celebrate.
NASB.25: 'Now his older son was in the field, and when he came and approached the house, he heard music and dancing.
NASB.26: 'And he summoned one of the servants and [began] inquiring what these things could be.
NASB.27: 'And he said to him, 'Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fattened calf because he has received him back safe and sound.'
NASB.28: 'But he became angry and was not willing to go in; and his father came out and [began] pleading with him.
NASB.29: 'But he answered and said to his father, 'Look! For so many years I have been serving you and I have never neglected a command of yours; and [yet] you have never given me a young goat, so that I might celebrate with my friends;
NASB.30: but when this son of yours came, who has devoured your wealth with prostitutes, you killed the fattened calf for him.'
NASB.31: 'And he said to him, 'Son, you have always been with me, and all that is mine is yours.
NASB.32: 'But we had to celebrate and rejoice, for this brother of yours was dead and [has begun] to live, and [was] lost and has been found.''

NLT.1: Tax collectors and other notorious sinners often came to listen to Jesus teach.
NLT.2: This made the Pharisees and teachers of religious law complain that he was associating with such sinful people — even eating with them!
NLT.3: So Jesus told them this story:
NLT.4: “If a man has a hundred sheep and one of them gets lost, what will he do? Won’t he leave the ninety-nine others in the wilderness and go to search for the one that is lost until he finds it?
NLT.5: And when he has found it, he will joyfully carry it home on his shoulders.
NLT.6: When he arrives, he will call together his friends and neighbors, saying, ‘Rejoice with me because I have found my lost sheep.’
NLT.7: In the same way, there is more joy in heaven over one lost sinner who repents and returns to God than over ninety-nine others who are righteous and haven’t strayed away!
NLT.8: “Or suppose a woman has ten silver coins and loses one. Won’t she light a lamp and sweep the entire house and search carefully until she finds it?
NLT.9: And when she finds it, she will call in her friends and neighbors and say, ‘Rejoice with me because I have found my lost coin.’
NLT.10: In the same way, there is joy in the presence of God’s angels when even one sinner repents.”
NLT.11: To illustrate the point further, Jesus told them this story: “A man had two sons.
NLT.12: The younger son told his father, ‘I want my share of your estate now before you die.’ So his father agreed to divide his wealth between his sons.
NLT.13: “A few days later this younger son packed all his belongings and moved to a distant land, and there he wasted all his money in wild living.
NLT.14: About the time his money ran out, a great famine swept over the land, and he began to starve.
NLT.15: He persuaded a local farmer to hire him, and the man sent him into his fields to feed the pigs.
NLT.16: The young man became so hungry that even the pods he was feeding the pigs looked good to him. But no one gave him anything.
NLT.17: “When he finally came to his senses, he said to himself, ‘At home even the hired servants have food enough to spare, and here I am dying of hunger!
NLT.18: I will go home to my father and say, “Father, I have sinned against both heaven and you,
NLT.19: and I am no longer worthy of being called your son. Please take me on as a hired servant.”’
NLT.20: “So he returned home to his father. And while he was still a long way off, his father saw him coming. Filled with love and compassion, he ran to his son, embraced him, and kissed him.
NLT.21: His son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against both heaven and you, and I am no longer worthy of being called your son. ’
NLT.22: “But his father said to the servants, ‘Quick! Bring the finest robe in the house and put it on him. Get a ring for his finger and sandals for his feet.
NLT.23: And kill the calf we have been fattening. We must celebrate with a feast,
NLT.24: for this son of mine was dead and has now returned to life. He was lost, but now he is found.’ So the party began.
NLT.25: “Meanwhile, the older son was in the fields working. When he returned home, he heard music and dancing in the house,
NLT.26: and he asked one of the servants what was going on.
NLT.27: ‘Your brother is back,’ he was told, ‘and your father has killed the fattened calf. We are celebrating because of his safe return.’
NLT.28: “The older brother was angry and wouldn’t go in. His father came out and begged him,
NLT.29: but he replied, ‘All these years I’ve slaved for you and never once refused to do a single thing you told me to. And in all that time you never gave me even one young goat for a feast with my friends.
NLT.30: Yet when this son of yours comes back after squandering your money on prostitutes, you celebrate by killing the fattened calf!’
NLT.31: “His father said to him, ‘Look, dear son, you have always stayed by me, and everything I have is yours.
NLT.32: We had to celebrate this happy day. For your brother was dead and has come back to life! He was lost, but now he is found!’”

WEB.1: Now all the tax collectors and sinners were coming close to him to hear him.
WEB.2: The Pharisees and the scribes murmured, saying, “This man welcomes sinners, and eats with them.”
WEB.4: “Which of you men, if you had one hundred sheep, and lost one of them, wouldn’t leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness, and go after the one that was lost, until he found it?
WEB.5: When he has found it, he carries it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
WEB.6: When he comes home, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep which was lost!’
WEB.7: I tell you that even so there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents, than over ninety-nine righteous people who need no repentance.
WEB.8: Or what woman, if she had ten drachma  coins, if she lost one drachma coin, wouldn’t light a lamp, sweep the house, and seek diligently until she found it?
WEB.9: When she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found the drachma which I had lost.’
WEB.10: Even so, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner repenting.”
WEB.11: He said, “A certain man had two sons.
WEB.12: The younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me my share of your property.’ He divided his livelihood between them.
WEB.13: Not many days after, the younger son gathered all of this together and traveled into a far country. There he wasted his property with riotous living.
WEB.14: When he had spent all of it, there arose a severe famine in that country, and he began to be in need.
WEB.15: He went and joined himself to one of the citizens of that country, and he sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
WEB.16: He wanted to fill his belly with the husks that the pigs ate, but no one gave him any.
WEB.17: But when he came to himself he said, ‘How many hired servants of my father’s have bread enough to spare, and I’m dying with hunger!
WEB.18: I will get up and go to my father, and will tell him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in your sight.
WEB.19: I am no more worthy to be called your son. Make me as one of your hired servants.”’
WEB.20: “He arose, and came to his father. But while he was still far off, his father saw him, and was moved with compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.
WEB.21: The son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in your sight. I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’
WEB.22: “But the father said to his servants, ‘Bring out the best robe, and put it on him. Put a ring on his hand, and shoes on his feet.
WEB.23: Bring the fattened calf, kill it, and let us eat, and celebrate;
WEB.24: for this, my son, was dead, and is alive again. He was lost, and is found.’ They began to celebrate.
WEB.25: “Now his elder son was in the field. As he came near to the house, he heard music and dancing.
WEB.26: He called one of the servants to him, and asked what was going on.
WEB.27: He said to him, ‘Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fattened calf, because he has received him back safe and healthy.’
WEB.28: But he was angry, and would not go in. Therefore his father came out, and begged him.
WEB.29: But he answered his father, ‘Behold, these many years I have served you, and I never disobeyed a commandment of yours, but you never gave me a goat, that I might celebrate with my friends.
WEB.30: But when this, your son, came, who has devoured your living with prostitutes, you killed the fattened calf for him.’
WEB.31: “He said to him, ‘Son, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.
WEB.32: But it was appropriate to celebrate and be glad, for this, your brother, was dead, and is alive again. He was lost, and is found.’”

CSB17.1: All <sup>ⓛ</sup> the tax collectors <sup>ⓜ</sup> and sinners were approaching to listen to him.
CSB17.2: And the Pharisees and scribes were complaining, “This man welcomes sinners <sup>ⓝ</sup> and eats with them.”
CSB17.3: So he told them this parable: <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.4: “What man among you, who has a hundred sheep and loses one of them, <sup>ⓑ</sup>does not leave the ninety-nine in the open field <sup>[1]</sup>and go after the lost one until he finds it?
CSB17.5: When he has found it, he joyfully puts it on his shoulders,
CSB17.6: and coming home, he calls his friends and neighbors together, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, because I have found my lost sheep!’
CSB17.7: I tell you, in the same way, there will be more joy in heaven <sup>ⓒ</sup>over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous people who don’t need repentance.
CSB17.8: “Or what woman who has ten silver coins, <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>[3]</sup> if she loses one coin, does not light a lamp, sweep the house, and search carefully until she finds it?
CSB17.9: When she finds it, she calls her friends and neighbors together, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, because I have found the silver coin I lost!’
CSB17.10: I tell you, in the same way, there is joy in the presence of God’s angels <sup>ⓓ</sup>over one sinner who repents.”
CSB17.11: He also said: “A man had two sons.
CSB17.12: The younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me the share of the estate I have coming to me.’ So he distributed the assets <sup>[4]</sup>to them.
CSB17.13: Not many days later, the younger son gathered together all he had and traveled to a distant country, where he squandered <sup>ⓔ</sup>his estate in foolish living. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.14: After he had spent everything, a severe famine struck that country, and he had nothing. <sup>[5]</sup>
CSB17.15: Then he went to work for one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.16: He longed to eat his fill from <sup>[6]</sup>the pods that the pigs were eating, but no one would give him anything.
CSB17.17: When he came to his senses, <sup>[7]</sup>he said, ‘How many of my father’s hired workers have more than enough food, and here I am dying of hunger! <sup>[8]</sup>
CSB17.18: I’ll get up, go to my father, and say to him, “Father, I have sinned <sup>ⓗ</sup>against heaven <sup>ⓘ</sup>and in your sight.
CSB17.19: I’m no longer worthy <sup>ⓙ</sup>to be called your son. Make me like one of your hired workers.”’
CSB17.20: So he got up and went to his father. But while the son was still a long way off, his father saw him and was filled with compassion. <sup>ⓚ</sup>He ran, threw his arms around his neck, and kissed <sup>ⓛ</sup>him.
CSB17.21: The son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and in your sight. I’m no longer worthy to be called your son.’
CSB17.22: “But the father told his servants, ‘Quick! Bring out the best robe <sup>ⓜ</sup> and put it on him; put a ring <sup>ⓝ</sup> on his finger and sandals <sup>ⓐ</sup> on his feet.
CSB17.23: Then bring the fattened calf <sup>ⓑ</sup>and slaughter it, and let’s celebrate with a feast,
CSB17.24: because this son of mine was dead and is alive again;  <sup>ⓒ</sup>he was lost and is found! ’ So they began to celebrate.
CSB17.25: “Now his older son was in the field; as he came near the house, he heard music and dancing.
CSB17.26: So he summoned one of the servants, questioning what these things meant.
CSB17.27: ‘Your brother is here,’ he told him, ‘and your father has slaughtered the fattened calf because he has him back safe and sound.’ <sup>[9]</sup>
CSB17.28: “Then he became angry and didn’t want to go in. So his father came out and pleaded with him.
CSB17.29: But he replied to his father, ‘Look, I have been slaving many years for you, and I have never disobeyed your orders, yet you never gave me a goat so that I could celebrate with my friends.
CSB17.30: But when this son of yours came, who has devoured your assets <sup>[10]</sup>with prostitutes, <sup>ⓓ</sup>you slaughtered the fattened calf for him.’
CSB17.31: “‘Son,’ <sup>[11]</sup> he said to him, ‘you are always with me, <sup>ⓔ</sup> and everything I have is yours.
CSB17.32: But we had to celebrate and rejoice, because this brother of yours was dead and is alive again; he was lost and is found.’”

AMP.1: Now all the tax collectors and sinners [including non-observant Jews] were coming near Jesus to listen to Him.
AMP.2: Both the Pharisees and the scribes began muttering and complaining, saying, "This man accepts and welcomes sinners and eats with them."
AMP.3: So He told them this parable:
AMP.4: "What man among you, if he has a hundred sheep and loses one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness and go after the one which is lost, [searching] until he finds it?
AMP.5: And when he has found it, he lays it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
AMP.6: And when he gets home, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, 'Rejoice with me, because I have found my lost sheep!'
AMP.7: I tell you, in the same way there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous people who have no need of repentance.
AMP.8: "Or what woman, if she has ten silver coins [each one equal to a day's wages] and loses one coin, does not light a lamp and sweep the house and search carefully until she finds it?
AMP.9: And when she has found it, she calls together her [women] friends and neighbors, saying, 'Rejoice with me, because I found the lost coin!'
AMP.10: In the same way, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner who repents [that is, changes his inner self—his old way of thinking, regrets past sins, lives his life in a way that proves repentance; and seeks God's purpose for his life]."
AMP.11: Then He said, "A certain man had two sons.
AMP.12: The younger of them [inappropriately] said to his father, 'Father, give me the share of the property that falls to me.' So he divided the estate between them.
AMP.13: A few days later, the younger son gathered together everything [that he had] and traveled to a distant country, and there he wasted his fortune in reckless and immoral living.
AMP.14: Now when he had spent everything, a severe famine occurred in that country, and he began to do without and be in need.
AMP.15: So he went and forced himself on one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
AMP.16: He would have gladly eaten the [carob] pods that the pigs were eating [but they could not satisfy his hunger], and no one was giving anything to him.
AMP.17: But when he [finally] came to his senses, he said, 'How many of my father's hired men have more than enough food, while I am dying here of hunger!
AMP.18: I will get up and go to my father, and I will say to him, "Father, I have sinned against heaven and in your sight.
AMP.19: I am no longer worthy to be called your son; [just] treat me like one of your hired men."'
AMP.20: So he got up and came to his father. But while he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was moved with compassion for him, and ran and embraced him and kissed him.
AMP.21: And the son said to him, 'Father, I have sinned against heaven and in your sight; I am no longer worthy to be called your son.'
AMP.22: But the father said to his servants, 'Quickly bring out the best robe [for the guest of honor] and put it on him; and give him a ring for his hand, and sandals for his feet.
AMP.23: And bring the fattened calf and slaughter it, and let us [invite everyone and] feast and celebrate;
AMP.24: for this son of mine was [as good as] dead and is alive again; he was lost and has been found.' So they began to celebrate.
AMP.25: "Now his older son was in the field; and when he returned and approached the house, he heard music and dancing.
AMP.26: So he summoned one of the servants and began asking what this [celebration] meant.
AMP.27: And he said to him, 'Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fattened calf because he has received him back safe and sound.'
AMP.28: But the elder brother became angry and deeply resentful and was not willing to go in; and his father came out and began pleading with him.
AMP.29: But he said to his father, 'Look! These many years I have served you, and I have never neglected or disobeyed your command. Yet you have never given me [so much as] a young goat, so that I might celebrate with my friends;
AMP.30: but when this [other] son of yours arrived, who has devoured your estate with immoral women, you slaughtered that fattened calf for him!'
AMP.31: The father said to him, 'Son, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.
AMP.32: But it was fitting to celebrate and rejoice, for this brother of yours was [as good as] dead and has begun to live. He was lost and has been found.'"

MSG.1: By this time a lot of men and women of doubtful reputation were hanging around Jesus, listening intently.
MSG.2: The Pharisees and religion scholars were not pleased, not at all pleased. They growled, "He takes in sinners and eats meals with them, treating them like old friends."
MSG.4: "Suppose one of you had a hundred sheep and lost one. Wouldn't you leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness and go after the lost one until you found it?
MSG.5: When found, you can be sure you would put it across your shoulders, rejoicing,
MSG.6: and when you got home call in your friends and neighbors, saying, 'Celebrate with me! I've found my lost sheep!'
MSG.7: Count on it—there's more joy in heaven over one sinner's rescued life than over ninety-nine good people in no need of rescue.
MSG.8: "Or imagine a woman who has ten coins and loses one. Won't she light a lamp and scour the house, looking in every nook and cranny until she finds it?
MSG.9: And when she finds it you can be sure she'll call her friends and neighbors: 'Celebrate with me! I found my lost coin!'
MSG.10: Count on it—that's the kind of party God's angels throw every time one lost soul turns to God."
MSG.11: Then he said, "There was once a man who had two sons.
MSG.12: The younger said to his father, 'Father, I want right now what's coming to me.' "So the father divided the property between them.
MSG.13: It wasn't long before the younger son packed his bags and left for a distant country. There, undisciplined and dissipated, he wasted everything he had.
MSG.14: After he had gone through all his money, there was a bad famine all through that country and he began to hurt.
MSG.15: He signed on with a citizen there who assigned him to his fields to slop the pigs.
MSG.16: He was so hungry he would have eaten the corncobs in the pig slop, but no one would give him any.
MSG.17: "That brought him to his senses. He said, 'All those farmhands working for my father sit down to three meals a day, and here I am starving to death.
MSG.18: I'm going back to my father. I'll say to him, Father, I've sinned against God, I've sinned before you;
MSG.19: I don't deserve to be called your son. Take me on as a hired hand.'
MSG.20: He got right up and went home to his father. "When he was still a long way off, his father saw him. His heart pounding, he ran out, embraced him, and kissed him.
MSG.21: The son started his speech: 'Father, I've sinned against God, I've sinned before you; I don't deserve to be called your son ever again.'
MSG.22: "But the father wasn't listening. He was calling to the servants, 'Quick. Bring a clean set of clothes and dress him. Put the family ring on his finger and sandals on his feet.
MSG.23: Then get a grain-fed heifer and roast it. We're going to feast! We're going to have a wonderful time!
MSG.24: My son is here—given up for dead and now alive! Given up for lost and now found!' And they began to have a wonderful time.
MSG.25: "All this time his older son was out in the field. When the day's work was done he came in. As he approached the house, he heard the music and dancing.
MSG.26: Calling over one of the houseboys, he asked what was going on.
MSG.27: He told him, 'Your brother came home. Your father has ordered a feast—barbecued beef!—because he has him home safe and sound.'
MSG.28: "The older brother stalked off in an angry sulk and refused to join in. His father came out and tried to talk to him, but he wouldn't listen.
MSG.29: The son said, 'Look how many years I've stayed here serving you, never giving you one moment of grief, but have you ever thrown a party for me and my friends?
MSG.30: Then this son of yours who has thrown away your money on whores shows up and you go all out with a feast!'
MSG.31: "His father said, 'Son, you don't understand. You're with me all the time, and everything that is mine is yours—
MSG.32: but this is a wonderful time, and we had to celebrate. This brother of yours was dead, and he's alive! He was lost, and he's found!'"

NIV.1:
The Parable of the Lost Sheep
Now the tax collectors and “sinners” were all gathering around to hear him.
NIV.2: But the Pharisees and the teachers of the law muttered, “This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.”
NIV.3: Then Jesus told them this parable:
NIV.4: “Suppose one of you has a hundred sheep and loses one of them. Does he not leave the ninety-nine in the open country and go after the lost sheep until he finds it?
NIV.5: And when he finds it, he joyfully puts it on his shoulders
NIV.6: and goes home. Then he calls his friends and neighbors together and says, ‘Rejoice with me; I have found my lost sheep.’
NIV.7: I tell you that in the same way there will be more rejoicing in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous persons who do not need to repent.
NIV.8:
The Parable of the Lost Coin
“Or suppose a woman has ten silver coins and loses one. Does she not light a lamp, sweep the house and search carefully until she finds it?
NIV.9: And when she finds it, she calls her friends and neighbors together and says, ‘Rejoice with me; I have found my lost coin.’
NIV.10: In the same way, I tell you, there is rejoicing in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner who repents.”
NIV.11:
The Parable of the Lost Son
Jesus continued: “There was a man who had two sons.
NIV.12: The younger one said to his father, ‘Father, give me my share of the estate.’ So he divided his property between them.
NIV.13: “Not long after that, the younger son got together all he had, set off for a distant country and there squandered his wealth in wild living.
NIV.14: After he had spent everything, there was a severe famine in that whole country, and he began to be in need.
NIV.15: So he went and hired himself out to a citizen of that country, who sent him to his fields to feed pigs.
NIV.16: He longed to fill his stomach with the pods that the pigs were eating, but no one gave him anything.
NIV.17: “When he came to his senses, he said, ‘How many of my father’s hired men have food to spare, and here I am starving to death!
NIV.18: I will set out and go back to my father and say to him: Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
NIV.19: I am no longer worthy to be called your son; make me like one of your hired men.’
NIV.20:
So he got up and went to his father.
“But while he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was filled with compassion for him; he ran to his son, threw his arms around him and kissed him.
NIV.21: “The son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’
NIV.22: “But the father said to his servants, ‘Quick! Bring the best robe and put it on him. Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet.
NIV.23: Bring the fattened calf and kill it. Let’s have a feast and celebrate.
NIV.24: For this son of mine was dead and is alive again; he was lost and is found.’ So they began to celebrate.
NIV.25: “Meanwhile, the older son was in the field. When he came near the house, he heard music and dancing.
NIV.26: So he called one of the servants and asked him what was going on.
NIV.27: ‘Your brother has come,’ he replied, ‘and your father has killed the fattened calf because he has him back safe and sound.’
NIV.28: “The older brother became angry and refused to go in. So his father went out and pleaded with him.
NIV.29: But he answered his father, ‘Look! All these years I’ve been slaving for you and never disobeyed your orders. Yet you never gave me even a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.
NIV.30: But when this son of yours who has squandered your property with prostitutes comes home, you kill the fattened calf for him!’
NIV.31: “‘My son,’ the father said, ‘you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
NIV.32: But we had to celebrate and be glad, because this brother of yours was dead and is alive again; he was lost and is found.’”

NRSVCE.1: Now all the tax collectors and sinners were coming near to listen to him.
NRSVCE.2: And the Pharisees and the scribes were grumbling and saying, “This fellow welcomes sinners and eats with them.”
NRSVCE.3: So he told them this parable:
NRSVCE.4: “Which one of you, having a hundred sheep and losing one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness and go after the one that is lost until he finds it?
NRSVCE.5: When he has found it, he lays it on his shoulders and rejoices.
NRSVCE.6: And when he comes home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep that was lost.’
NRSVCE.7: Just so, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous persons who need no repentance.
NRSVCE.8: “Or what woman having ten silver coins, if she loses one of them, does not light a lamp, sweep the house, and search carefully until she finds it?
NRSVCE.9: When she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found the coin that I had lost.’
NRSVCE.10: Just so, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner who repents.”
NRSVCE.11: Then Jesus said, “There was a man who had two sons.
NRSVCE.12: The younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me the share of the property that will belong to me.’ So he divided his property between them.
NRSVCE.13: A few days later the younger son gathered all he had and traveled to a distant country, and there he squandered his property in dissolute living.
NRSVCE.14: When he had spent everything, a severe famine took place throughout that country, and he began to be in need.
NRSVCE.15: So he went and hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him to his fields to feed the pigs.
NRSVCE.16: He would gladly have filled himself with the pods that the pigs were eating; and no one gave him anything.
NRSVCE.17: But when he came to himself he said, ‘How many of my father’s hired hands have bread enough and to spare, but here I am dying of hunger!
NRSVCE.18: I will get up and go to my father, and I will say to him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven and before you;
NRSVCE.19: I am no longer worthy to be called your son; treat me like one of your hired hands.”’
NRSVCE.20: So he set off and went to his father. But while he was still far off, his father saw him and was filled with compassion; he ran and put his arms around him and kissed him.
NRSVCE.21: Then the son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and before you; I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’
NRSVCE.22: But the father said to his slaves, ‘Quickly, bring out a robe — the best one — and put it on him; put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet.
NRSVCE.23: And get the fatted calf and kill it, and let us eat and celebrate;
NRSVCE.24: for this son of mine was dead and is alive again; he was lost and is found!’ And they began to celebrate.
NRSVCE.25: “Now his elder son was in the field; and when he came and approached the house, he heard music and dancing.
NRSVCE.26: He called one of the slaves and asked what was going on.
NRSVCE.27: He replied, ‘Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fatted calf, because he has got him back safe and sound.’
NRSVCE.28: Then he became angry and refused to go in. His father came out and began to plead with him.
NRSVCE.29: But he answered his father, ‘Listen! For all these years I have been working like a slave for you, and I have never disobeyed your command; yet you have never given me even a young goat so that I might celebrate with my friends.
NRSVCE.30: But when this son of yours came back, who has devoured your property with prostitutes, you killed the fatted calf for him!’
NRSVCE.31: Then the father said to him, ‘Son, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.
NRSVCE.32: But we had to celebrate and rejoice, because this brother of yours was dead and has come to life; he was lost and has been found.’”

NET.1: Now all the tax collectors and sinners were coming to hear him.
NET.2: But the Pharisees and the experts in the law were complaining, “This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.”
NET.3: So Jesus told them this parable:
NET.4: “Which one of you, if he has a hundred sheep and loses one of them, would not leave the ninety-nine in the open pasture and go look for the one that is lost until he finds it?
NET.5: Then when he has found it, he places it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
NET.6: Returning home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, telling them, ‘Rejoice with me, because I have found my sheep that was lost.’
NET.7: I tell you, in the same way there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous people who have no need to repent.
NET.8: “Or what woman, if she has ten silver coins and loses one of them, does not light a lamp, sweep the house, and search thoroughly until she finds it?
NET.9: Then when she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found the coin that I had lost.’
NET.10: In the same way, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of God’s angels over one sinner who repents.”
NET.11: Then Jesus said, “A man had two sons.
NET.12: The younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me the share of the estate that will belong to me.’ So he divided his assets between them.
NET.13: After a few days, the younger son gathered together all he had and left on a journey to a distant country, and there he squandered his wealth with a wild lifestyle.
NET.14: Then after he had spent everything, a severe famine took place in that country, and he began to be in need.
NET.15: So he went and worked for one of the citizens of that country, who sent him to his fields to feed pigs.
NET.16: He was longing to eat the carob pods the pigs were eating, but no one gave him anything.
NET.17: But when he came to his senses he said, ‘How many of my father’s hired workers have food enough to spare, but here I am dying from hunger!
NET.18: I will get up and go to my father and say to him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
NET.19: I am no longer worthy to be called your son; treat me like one of your hired workers.”’
NET.20: So he got up and went to his father. But while he was still a long way from home his father saw him, and his heart went out to him; he ran and hugged his son and kissed him.
NET.21: Then his son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you; I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’
NET.22: But the father said to his slaves, ‘Hurry! Bring the best robe, and put it on him! Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet!
NET.23: Bring the fattened calf and kill it! Let us eat and celebrate,
NET.24: because this son of mine was dead, and is alive again — he was lost and is found!’ So they began to celebrate.
NET.25: “Now his older son was in the field. As he came and approached the house, he heard music and dancing.
NET.26: So he called one of the slaves and asked what was happening.
NET.27: The slave replied, ‘Your brother has returned, and your father has killed the fattened calf because he got his son back safe and sound.’
NET.28: But the older son became angry and refused to go in. His father came out and appealed to him,
NET.29: but he answered his father, ‘Look! These many years I have worked like a slave for you, and I never disobeyed your commands. Yet you never gave me even a goat so that I could celebrate with my friends!
NET.30: But when this son of yours came back, who has devoured your assets with prostitutes, you killed the fattened calf for him!’
NET.31: Then the father said to him, ‘Son, you are always with me, and everything that belongs to me is yours.
NET.32: It was appropriate to celebrate and be glad, for your brother was dead, and is alive; he was lost and is found.’”
